name: Plasmapheresis
description: Plasmapheresis / therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) — extracorporeal removal (and replacement) of plasma.

en.name: Plasmapheresis
en.shortDescription: Plasmapheresis / therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) — extracorporeal removal (and replacement) of plasma.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Plasmapheresis is an extracorporeal procedure in which blood is separated and the plasma is removed (and replaced with a substitute fluid or returned after treatment), used to remove circulating antibodies, immune complexes or toxins — e.g. in autoimmune and certain neurological or haematological conditions. Therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) is the form in which the removed plasma is replaced. In source data this is a **procedure** record; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care, nephrology and haematology.

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED procedure *Plasmapheresis* with its descendants.

- *4049372 / 20720000 — Plasmapheresis (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default.
- *4076662 / 225067007 — Therapeutic plasma exchange (SNOMED)*: plasmapheresis with replacement of the removed plasma (TPE).
- *4055766 / 19647005 — Therapeutic plasmapheresis using plasma as the major replacement fluid (SNOMED)*.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records plasmapheresis or plasma exchange without detail: *4049372 / 20720000 — Plasmapheresis*. Map to *4076662 / 225067007 — Therapeutic plasma exchange* when the source specifies plasma exchange (TPE).
- **Not other apheresis / RRT**: cytapheresis and renal replacement therapy (haemodialysis/haemofiltration) are different extracorporeal procedures in their own concept sets.

fr.name: Plasmaphérèse
fr.shortDescription: Plasmaphérèse / échange plasmatique thérapeutique (EP) — retrait (et remplacement) extracorporel du plasma.

versions.message: Anchor on Plasmapheresis (4049372) with descendants (therapeutic plasma exchange, therapeutic plasmapheresis). Fixed FR name to "Plasmaphérèse". Added description.
